meaning: vt.使纠缠,使混乱,解开,弄清vi.散开,解决n.错综复杂的一团
examples: He was trying to ravel out the complicated series of events that had led to this situation.
We have to ravel the matter out.
Can ravel out what's in my head.
"Having seen and studied all these manuscripts, I kind of sense how Ravel thinks, " Mr. Orenstein says.
Sautet builds up a needling tension, aided by fine renditions of Ravel at his most difficult and plaintive.
